run_metadata.notes: Inputs copied from Desktop lyra/lyra:lyra/ → audits/lyra-agent-outputs/. Progress update: 22 findings resolved (auth, RLS, CI, error boundaries, health, worker stuck-job, run payload, deploy docs, failed-projects UX; + empty catch, loadProject/listAllProjects parse, undefined name, resolveApps dead branch, ProjectView setSelected, orchestration silent failure, job_type CHECK).

agent.name: synthesizer
agent.role: Merge, deduplicate, rank, and plan across all agent outputs.

coverage.incomplete_reason: logic: Full per-file scan of all dashboard app/api/lib/components not completed; worker src fully reviewed.; performance: Did not exhaust every API route or static asset; focused on hot paths (projects API, worker job loop, LLM calls, orchestration fetches).; security: Worker process, hosting-specific hardening, and all client components for secret handling not exhaustively reviewed; CORS assumed Next defaults.

findings.title: Supabase tables lyra_* created with no RLS or policies
findings.description: Migration 20250318120000_lyra_core.sql defines public.lyra_projects, lyra_audit_jobs, and lyra_audit_runs but never enables ROW LEVEL SECURITY or creates policies. On Supabase, any client using the anon key with PostgREST (or mis-scoped service usage) could read/write all rows. Current dashboard/worker use a direct Postgres pool with DATABASE_URL, which bypasses RLS, so enforcement is entirely application-side.

proof_hooks.summary: Migration ends after CREATE TABLE/INDEX/COMMENT; no ALTER TABLE … ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY or CREATE POLICY.
Server uses raw SQL pool, not JWT-scoped Supabase client.

findings.impact: Cross-tenant or public exposure if tables are ever queried with a role subject to RLS defaults, or if anon/service keys leak; no database-level isolation for multi-tenant scenarios.

suggested_fix.approach: Enable RLS on all three tables; add policies aligned to your auth model (e.g. service_role-only for worker/dashboard, or auth.uid() if user-scoped). If intentionally server-only, document and restrict DB roles so anon cannot SELECT/INSERT.
tests_needed: Policy tests with Supabase SQL or integration test using anon vs service role

history.notes: Added supabase/migrations/20260319120000_lyra_rls.sql: RLS enabled on lyra_projects, lyra_audit_jobs, lyra_audit_runs; no policies so anon/authenticated PostgREST blocked.

findings.title: lyra_audit_jobs.project_name not FK-referenced to lyra_projects
findings.description: Column project_name is free text. Jobs and runs can reference deleted or never-existing projects; worker loadProject then returns null while job rows still claim a project_name.

proof_hooks.summary: lyra_audit_jobs defines project_name text without REFERENCES public.lyra_projects(name).
proof_hooks.expected_schema: project_name REFERENCES lyra_projects(name) ON DELETE SET NULL or RESTRICT

proof_hooks.summary: Jobs inserted with arbitrary project_name string.

findings.impact: Orphan job references, confusing orchestration UI, and worker behavior when project missing.

suggested_fix.approach: Add FK from lyra_audit_jobs.project_name to lyra_projects(name) with ON DELETE SET NULL; optionally same for lyra_audit_runs.project_name; backfill invalid names before migration.

history.notes: Added supabase/migrations/20260319150000_lyra_project_name_fk.sql: backfill orphan project_name, FK on lyra_audit_jobs and lyra_audit_runs to lyra_projects(name) ON DELETE SET NULL.

findings.title: LyraAuditRunRow omits payload present on lyra_audit_runs
findings.description: Table lyra_audit_runs includes payload jsonb NOT NULL. TypeScript LyraAuditRunRow and rowRun() do not expose payload, so consumers of listRecentAuditRuns lose typed access to run payload despite SELECT *.

proof_hooks.summary: Row mapper drops payload column.
Migration defines payload on lyra_audit_runs.

findings.impact: API/UI may need run payload later; hidden field encourages ad-hoc queries or casts.

suggested_fix.approach: Add payload: Record<string, unknown> to LyraAuditRunRow and map in rowRun.

history.notes: LyraAuditRunRow.payload added; rowRun() maps payload from SELECT *.

findings.title: job_type unrestricted in DB vs LyraJobType union in code
findings.description: Postgres allows any text for job_type; application restricts to a union. Invalid values can be inserted via SQL or bugs, breaking worker switches.

proof_hooks.summary: lyra_audit_jobs.job_type is text NOT NULL without CHECK constraint.
LyraJobType union in TS.

findings.impact: Low probability but causes runtime mis-handling of unknown job_type.

suggested_fix.approach: Add CHECK (job_type IN (...)) or Postgres ENUM matching LyraJobType; keep worker/dashboard in sync.

history.notes: Added supabase/migrations/20260319130000_lyra_job_type_check.sql: CHECK on lyra_audit_jobs and lyra_audit_runs job_type.

findings.title: No GitHub Actions (or other) CI pipeline
findings.description: Repository has no .github/workflows. README documents manual npm run lint/build locally only. There is no automated gate for lint, typecheck, or build on push/PR. Dashboard package.json defines lint and build but not typecheck; worker has build only.

proof_hooks.summary: No workflow YAML under .github/workflows
Dashboard scripts omit typecheck

findings.impact: Broken builds or type errors can reach Netlify/production without review; regressions discovered only after deploy or manual runs.

suggested_fix.approach: Add .github/workflows/ci.yml: checkout, setup Node, npm ci in dashboard/, run npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build; optionally worker build. Require on main/PR.
tests_needed: Confirm workflow fails on intentional TS error

history.notes: History lookup: open_findings.json had no prior ci-gap entries.
Added .github/workflows/ci.yml (dashboard lint, tsc --noEmit, build; worker build). dashboard/package.json typecheck script.

findings.title: Next.js App Router has no route or global error boundary
findings.description: dashboard/app has no error.tsx or global-error.tsx. Uncaught React errors in the App Router surface as default Next.js failure UX with no app-controlled fallback or recovery path.

proof_hooks.summary: No error.tsx under dashboard/app

findings.impact: Users see generic error states; no consistent branded recovery or error reporting hook from the UI tree.

suggested_fix.approach: Add dashboard/app/error.tsx and optionally dashboard/app/global-error.tsx with fallback UI and link home; wire to monitoring if added.

history.notes: Added dashboard/app/error.tsx and global-error.tsx with fallback UI and reset/link home.

findings.title: No dedicated health check endpoint for load balancers or uptime
findings.description: No /api/health or similar route found. Netlify and operators cannot cheaply verify app liveness separate from full page render.

proof_hooks.summary: No health route references under dashboard

findings.impact: Uptime monitors and deploy verification rely on full pages; DB/API failures may be harder to distinguish from static success.

suggested_fix.approach: Add GET /api/health returning 200 with optional lightweight DB ping behind flag.

history.notes: Added GET /api/health returning { ok: true, service: "lyra-dashboard" }; public, no auth.

findings.title: Empty catch blocks swallow errors in lyra-dashboard.jsx
findings.description: Two catch (e) {} handlers discard exceptions with no log, hiding failures during dashboard JSX flows.

proof_hooks.summary: catch (e) {} with no body

findings.impact: Silent failures in that UI path; difficult to debug production or local issues.

suggested_fix.approach: Log with console.error or structured logger; rethrow or set user-visible error state.

history.notes: Added console.error in both catch blocks (load/save projects).

findings.title: No external error/performance monitoring on dashboard or worker
findings.description: Logging is ad hoc console.error in API routes and worker. No Sentry, OpenTelemetry, or similar appears in dependencies or config, so production errors lack aggregation, alerts, or traces.

proof_hooks.summary: Representative API error logging only to console
dashboard/package.json has no observability SDK

findings.impact: Production incidents require log diving; no correlated user sessions or alerting.

suggested_fix.approach: Add Sentry (or similar) for Next.js API + client; optional worker process wrapper.

history.notes: Added @sentry/nextjs; instrumentation-client.ts, sentry.server.config.ts, sentry.edge.config.ts, instrumentation.ts; withSentryConfig in next.config; captureException in error.tsx and global-error.tsx. README documents SENTRY_DSN / NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN.

findings.title: Both netlify.toml and dashboard/vercel.json present — which is canonical?
findings.description: README and netlify.toml describe Netlify deployment from dashboard/. dashboard/vercel.json also configures Vercel-style build. Clarify primary host to avoid duplicate or divergent deploy config.

proof_hooks.summary: Netlify build from dashboard base
Vercel-oriented config coexists

findings.impact: Risk of maintaining two deploy paths or wrong assumptions in docs/CI.

suggested_fix.approach: Document single source of truth; remove or archive unused deploy config.

history.notes: README updated: Netlify canonical; dashboard/vercel.json optional/alternate.

findings.title: Worker can leave audit job stuck in running if loadPrompts fails after claim
findings.description: After claimJob sets status to running, loadPrompts(), repoRoot(), and payload reads run outside the try/catch. readFileSync in loadPrompts can throw (permissions, IO). No completeJob(failed) runs, so the row may remain running indefinitely.

proof_hooks.summary: try begins at 73; loadPrompts at 68 is outside try

repro_steps: Claim a queued job (normal flow).
Temporarily chmod 000 core_system_prompt or audits/prompts/audit-agent.md or inject a failing read.
Observe lyra_audit_jobs.status stays running with no finished_at/error.
tests_needed: Unit test: mock loadPrompts to throw after claimJob succeeds; assert completeJob called with failed status or job reset.
findings.impact: After claimJob sets status to running, loadPrompts(), repoRoot(), and payload reads run outside the try/catch. readFileSync in loadPrompts can throw (permissions, IO). No completeJob(failed) runs, so the row may remain running indefinitely.

suggested_fix.approach: Unit test: mock loadPrompts to throw after claimJob succeeds; assert completeJob called with failed status or job reset.

history.notes: Normalized logic agent output: hook_type on proof_hooks, history[], suggested_fix/impact.
process-job.ts: loadPrompts/repoRoot inside try; on throw call completeJob(failed); inner completeJob wrapped in try/catch.

findings.title: runOne swallows errors; stuck jobs if completeJob fails in processJob catch
findings.description: index.ts runOne catches all processJob errors and only logs. If processJob catch block fails while calling completeJob (e.g. DB down), the exception propagates to runOne and is swallowed—job may stay running. Same for any unexpected throw outside processJob inner catch.

proof_hooks.summary: catch logs only, no rethrow or terminal state update
completeJob in catch can throw

repro_steps: With job in running state mid-process, kill DB or mock pool.query to reject on UPDATE.
Trigger failure path that reaches completeJob in catch.
Confirm worker logs error but job row not updated to failed.
tests_needed: Integration test: completeJob failure after audit failure still records failure or dead-letter.
findings.impact: index.ts runOne catches all processJob errors and only logs. If processJob catch block fails while calling completeJob (e.g. DB down), the exception propagates to runOne and is swallowed—job may stay running. Same for any unexpected throw outside processJob inner catch.

suggested_fix.approach: Integration test: completeJob failure after audit failure still records failure or dead-letter.

history.notes: Normalized logic agent output: hook_type on proof_hooks, history[], suggested_fix/impact.
index.ts runOne catch: on processJob throw, query job row and call completeJob(failed) if status still running; log completeJob failures.

findings.title: loadProject can throw on malformed project_json string
findings.description: When project_json is a string, JSON.parse(j) is unwrapped. Corrupt DB or manual edits cause worker/API using loadProject to crash.

proof_hooks.summary: JSON.parse without try/catch

repro_steps: INSERT invalid JSON string into lyra_projects.project_json for a row.
Invoke code path that loadProject (worker save/merge or any consumer).
tests_needed: Test loadProject returns null or safe empty project on parse error.
findings.impact: When project_json is a string, JSON.parse(j) is unwrapped. Corrupt DB or manual edits cause worker/API using loadProject to crash.

suggested_fix.approach: Test loadProject returns null or safe empty project on parse error.

history.notes: Normalized logic agent output: hook_type on proof_hooks, history[], suggested_fix/impact.
loadProject: try/catch around JSON.parse; return null and log on parse error. listAllProjects: try/catch per row, skip invalid; safe name fallback.

findings.title: listAllProjects may emit undefined project names
findings.description: j.name is used without fallback; malformed JSON yields undefined in synthesize portfolio lines.

proof_hooks.summary: name: j.name without default
p.name in template

repro_steps: Store project_json without name field.
Run synthesize_project; inspect LLM blob for 'undefined' entries.
tests_needed: listAllProjects coerces name to string or skips invalid rows.
findings.impact: j.name is used without fallback; malformed JSON yields undefined in synthesize portfolio lines.

suggested_fix.approach: listAllProjects coerces name to string or skips invalid rows.

history.notes: Normalized logic agent output: hook_type on proof_hooks, history[], suggested_fix/impact.
listAllProjects: name fallback (j?.name ?? 'unknown'), try/catch per row; skip invalid.

findings.description: jobType === 'synthesize_project' returns PORTFOLIO_APPS; final return is identical. synthesize path never calls resolveApps from processJob. Dead/unreachable in practice or redundant.

proof_hooks.summary: duplicate outcomes

findings.impact: jobType === 'synthesize_project' returns PORTFOLIO_APPS; final return is identical. synthesize path never calls resolveApps from processJob. Dead/unreachable in practice or redundant.

suggested_fix.approach: Address per description; add regression tests.

history.notes: Normalized logic agent output: hook_type on proof_hooks, history[], suggested_fix/impact.

findings.title: OrchestrationPanel may setState after unmount
findings.description: useEffect runs load() without abort; load() always calls setLoading/setData/setJobs at end. Rapid unmount triggers React warnings or stale updates. finally block only returns when cancelled and does not guard load internals.

proof_hooks.summary: Promise.all in load; no cancelled check before setState

repro_steps: Mount dashboard, navigate away before orchestration fetches complete.
Check console for React strict-mode setState on unmounted component warnings.
tests_needed: Component test with unmount during pending fetch; assert no setState after unmount.
findings.impact: useEffect runs load() without abort; load() always calls setLoading/setData/setJobs at end. Rapid unmount triggers React warnings or stale updates. finally block only returns when cancelled and does not guard load internals.

suggested_fix.approach: Component test with unmount during pending fetch; assert no setState after unmount.

history.notes: Normalized logic agent output: hook_type on proof_hooks, history[], suggested_fix/impact.
OrchestrationPanel: load() accepts optional AbortSignal; useEffect uses AbortController; guard setState after each await; cleanup aborts.

findings.description: After refetchProject(), setSelected uses findings.find from pre-await closure. Detail panel can show wrong merged fields if list changed server-side.

proof_hooks.summary: findings from closure vs updated.findings

repro_steps: Open finding detail, change status while another client edits same finding.
After action completes, verify panel shows server truth vs stale copy.
tests_needed: handleAction uses updated?.findings?.find after refetch for setSelected.
findings.impact: After refetchProject(), setSelected uses findings.find from pre-await closure. Detail panel can show wrong merged fields if list changed server-side.

suggested_fix.approach: handleAction uses updated?.findings?.find after refetch for setSelected.

history.notes: Normalized logic agent output: hook_type on proof_hooks, history[], suggested_fix/impact.
handleAction now uses updated?.findings?.find for setSelected after refetch.

findings.title: Orchestration panel hides entirely when GET /api/orchestration fails
findings.description: If orchestrationRes.ok is false, setData is never updated; loading ends with data still null; UI returns null—no error surface.

proof_hooks.summary: only setData on ok; !data return null

repro_steps: Break repo.list() or orchestration route (500).
Load dashboard; orchestration section absent without message.
tests_needed: E2E or component: failed orchestration fetch shows error state.
findings.impact: If orchestrationRes.ok is false, setData is never updated; loading ends with data still null; UI returns null—no error surface.

suggested_fix.approach: E2E or component: failed orchestration fetch shows error state.

history.notes: Normalized logic agent output: hook_type on proof_hooks, history[], suggested_fix/impact.
OrchestrationPanel: loadError state; show error UI when !orchestrationRes.ok.

findings.title: One OpenAI chat completion per app in a single audit job multiplies token cost
findings.description: processJob iterates resolveApps() and calls auditWithLlm once per app with full expectations + gatherCodeContext per iteration. A job that audits N apps issues N separate chat.completions requests with max_tokens 4096 each, scaling cost linearly with app count.

proof_hooks.summary: Sequential per-app LLM calls inside processJob loop
Each audit uses max_tokens 4096 and large user payload

findings.impact: Portfolio-wide or multi-app jobs can produce 3–10× API spend versus batched or single-pass strategies; also increases wall-clock time.

suggested_fix.approach: Batch apps into fewer calls (single prompt with section per app), cap apps per job, or use cheaper model for first pass with escalation.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Merged prompts may hit context limits; need chunking strategy.
tests_needed: Integration test with 2+ apps asserting call count or mock fetch assertions

findings.title: GET /api/projects returns every finding for every project with no pagination
findings.description: The portfolio page loads the full project list via repo.list(), serializing entire findings arrays for all projects on initial paint. Large portfolios mean large JSON over the wire and heavy client parse/render work.

proof_hooks.summary: API returns full project array from list()
Client fetches all projects on mount with no pagination

findings.impact: Slow TTI and memory use on dashboards with many projects or thousands of findings; repeated full refetches after mutations.

suggested_fix.approach: Add summary endpoint (counts, blockers only) for grid; lazy-load full findings per project on drill-in.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Requires repository interface changes for listSummaries vs listFull.
tests_needed: API contract test for summary shape

findings.title: synthesize_project loads full project_json for all rows
findings.description: listAllProjects runs SELECT project_json FROM lyra_projects with no LIMIT, parsing every project's full findings into memory. Only titles/severities are needed for the synthesis blob (and even that is capped to 200 lines after fetch).

proof_hooks.summary: Unbounded SELECT of all project JSON
Worker then slices to 200 lines after loading all findings
proof_hooks.expected_schema: SQL or RPC returning only finding_id, title, severity per project
proof_hooks.observed_schema: Full project_json blobs for N projects loaded into Node

findings.impact: DB memory/IO and Node heap spike as portfolio grows; wasted network from DB to worker.

suggested_fix.approach: Store denormalized finding summaries table or use JSONB path query / SQL to extract only needed fields with LIMIT per project.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: May need migration for summary columns.
tests_needed: Job with 2 projects verifies query uses projection or limit

findings.title: Worker polls lyra_audit_jobs on an interval when Redis is unset
findings.description: Without REDIS_URL, setInterval(poll, pollMs default 8000) issues SELECT ... WHERE status = queued repeatedly, creating steady DB QPS per worker instance.

proof_hooks.summary: Polling loop every LYRA_JOB_POLL_MS (default 8s)

findings.impact: Low per-query cost but continuous load; multiple workers multiply queries.

suggested_fix.approach: Document Redis for production; optionally use LISTEN/NOTIFY or longer idle backoff when queue empty.

history.notes: worker/README.md: production should set REDIS_URL to avoid DB QPS. worker/src/index.ts: idle backoff (LYRA_JOB_POLL_IDLE_MS, default 30s) when queue empty; poll only pollMs after a job ran.

findings.title: bullmq and ioredis in Next server dependencies may inflate serverless bundle
findings.description: dashboard depends on bullmq; orchestration jobs route imports Queue from bullmq. Serverless/edge deployments trace this into the API bundle, increasing cold start and deploy size relative to a thin HTTP enqueue to external Redis.

proof_hooks.summary: bullmq Queue used in API route
dashboard/package.json lists bullmq dependency

findings.impact: Larger Next server traces and slower cold starts on platforms that bundle API routes.

suggested_fix.approach: Measure next build trace; if large, move enqueue to worker-only or use minimal redis client.
tests_needed: Compare .next/server trace size before/after

findings.title: OrchestrationPanel issues three parallel fetches on every mount without cache or stale-while-revalidate
findings.description: load() hits /api/orchestration, /api/orchestration/jobs, /api/durable-state together on each panel mount. No SWR/React Query dedupe across navigations; user switching views may refetch identical data.

proof_hooks.summary: Promise.all of three internal APIs on load

findings.impact: Extra server load and latency when revisiting portfolio; minor compared to full-project payload issue.

suggested_fix.approach: Add SWR with dedupingInterval or lift orchestration state to a parent provider.

history.notes: OrchestrationPanel: 15s module-level cache for load(); remounts within window skip refetch.

findings.title: Confirm index on lyra_audit_jobs (status, created_at) for worker poll query
findings.description: Poll uses WHERE status = 'queued' ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1. Without a supporting index this may degrade to sequential scan as job history grows. Schema not verified in-repo.

proof_hooks.summary: Worker dequeue poll pattern
proof_hooks.query_text: SELECT id FROM lyra_audit_jobs WHERE status = 'queued' ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1

proof_hooks.summary: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on production-like data volume
proof_hooks.command: EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT id FROM lyra_audit_jobs WHERE status = 'queued' ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1;
proof_hooks.expected: Index Scan using partial or composite index on (status, created_at)
proof_hooks.actual: Unknown until run against populated DB

findings.impact: Possible latency spikes and CPU on large lyra_audit_jobs tables.

suggested_fix.approach: If Seq Scan, add CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY ... ON lyra_audit_jobs (created_at) WHERE status = 'queued';
tests_needed: EXPLAIN on staging

history.notes: Added supabase/migrations/20260319140000_lyra_queued_poll_index.sql: partial index on (created_at ASC) WHERE status = 'queued'.

findings.title: Dashboard APIs allow unauthenticated read/write of projects and findings
findings.description: Project and mutation routes call getRepository() with no session, API key, or middleware. Attack: anyone who can reach the deployed origin issues GET /api/projects to list every project and findings, PUT/DELETE to destroy or overwrite data, POST /api/import or POST /api/sync/audit to inject or merge findings, and POST/DELETE /api/engine/queue to manipulate the repair queue. No per-user isolation. Realistic when the dashboard is hosted on Netlify/Vercel without network restrictions.

proof_hooks.summary: GET/POST /api/projects have no auth before repo.list/create
PUT/DELETE project by name with no authorization
POST /api/import creates/updates projects from arbitrary JSON
POST /api/sync/audit merges filesystem findings into DB without auth
Engine queue readable/writable without auth
Unauthenticated read of all projects
steps: Deploy or run dashboard with DATABASE_URL set.
Observe 200 JSON with projects and findings without Authorization.

repro_steps: Run dashboard with DB containing projects.
curl -s <origin>/api/projects
Confirm full project and finding payload without credentials.
findings.impact: Full confidentiality and integrity loss for audit data; possible destruction of audit history.

suggested_fix.approach: Require Authorization: Bearer <ORCHESTRATION_ENQUEUE_SECRET> or DASHBOARD_API_SECRET on all /api/* except explicit health; or Netlify/Vercel auth + Next middleware. For multi-user, Supabase Auth + RLS on project rows.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Breaking for unauthenticated CI; document header for cron/automation.
tests_needed: Unauthenticated GET /api/projects → 401
Valid Bearer → 200

history.notes: open_findings.json was empty.

dashboard/middleware.ts: when DASHBOARD_API_SECRET or ORCHESTRATION_ENQUEUE_SECRET set, all /api/* require Bearer, x-lyra-api-secret, or session cookie; /api/health and POST /api/auth/login exempt. DashboardLogin + apiFetch(credentials:include). README updated.

findings.title: GET /api/orchestration/jobs exposes job and run history without authentication
findings.description: POST is protected when ORCHESTRATION_ENQUEUE_SECRET is set, but GET returns recent jobs and runs to any caller. Attack: reconnaissance on audit schedules and project names.

proof_hooks.summary: GET has no authorize()

findings.impact: Operational metadata leak; aids targeted abuse.

suggested_fix.approach: Apply same Bearer/header check as POST for GET.
tests_needed: GET without secret → 401 when secret configured

history.notes: Middleware protects all /api/* including GET /api/orchestration/jobs when secret configured.

findings.title: POST /api/sync/linear/push callable without auth; burns LINEAR_API_KEY
findings.description: Any client can POST with projectName to create/update Linear issues using the server LINEAR_API_KEY. Attack: spam Linear, exhaust rate limits, expose finding titles in a shared workspace.

proof_hooks.summary: POST validates Linear config and body only, not caller

findings.impact: Abuse of third-party API; unsolicited issues.

suggested_fix.approach: Require same API secret as other mutations before calling Linear APIs.
tests_needed: POST without auth → 401

history.notes: Middleware protects POST /api/sync/linear/push when secret configured.

findings.title: GET /api/durable-state returns orchestration config and events without auth
findings.description: Exposes durable state configuration and recent events to any caller.

proof_hooks.summary: No auth on GET

findings.impact: Information disclosure for orchestration behavior.

suggested_fix.approach: Gate with shared secret or session.

history.notes: Middleware protects GET /api/durable-state when secret configured.

findings.title: npm audit: Next.js moderate advisory (image cache disk growth)
findings.description: GHSA-3x4c-7xq6-9pq8: unbounded next/image cache. Risk mainly disk exhaustion on persistent Node hosts using next/image heavily.

proof_hooks.summary: npm audit reports 1 moderate next issue
next dependency range

findings.impact: Potential DoS via disk fill on long-running image-heavy deployments.

suggested_fix.approach: Upgrade Next to patched release per advisory when feasible.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Major version bump may need app changes.

history.notes: Upgraded dashboard next to ^16.1.7 (patched for GHSA-3x4c-7xq6-9pq8). npm audit no longer reports next moderate.

findings.title: API routes return raw Error.message to clients on 500
findings.description: Handlers often return e.message in JSON. May leak DB or path hints if errors are triggered.

proof_hooks.summary: 500 includes exception message

findings.impact: Minor information disclosure.

suggested_fix.approach: Generic client message in production; log server-side.

history.notes: Added dashboard/lib/api-error.ts (apiErrorMessage); all API routes return generic message in production, full message in dev.

findings.title: Failed /api/projects shows empty portfolio with no error
findings.description: fetchProjects only sets projects when res.ok; on network or 5xx failure the user sees the same empty state as a truly empty account ('No projects yet'), with only a console.error. No retry or error message.

proof_hooks.summary: Non-OK response leaves projects unchanged; finally clears loading
Load app with API down → misleading empty onboarding

repro_steps: Block or fail GET /api/projects (e.g. stop API or return 500).
Open /. Portfolio shows empty state as if there are no projects.
findings.impact: Users may believe data is lost or that onboarding is required; support burden and wrong actions.

suggested_fix.approach: Track fetch error state; show inline error with retry; distinguish empty vs error.
tests_needed: E2E or integration: mock 500 on /api/projects, assert error UI

history.notes: page.tsx: projectsError state; on !res.ok set message and show Retry; 401 triggers needsAuth (login).

findings.title: No React error boundary around main app shell
findings.description: Root layout renders children with no error boundary. Any uncaught render error in portfolio/engine/project view white-screens the full app with Next.js default error UI and no Lyra-branded recovery.

proof_hooks.summary: Layout wraps only body + children, no ErrorBoundary

findings.impact: Single component bug can remove all navigation and context; poor recovery UX.

suggested_fix.approach: Add app/error.tsx and/or segment error boundary with reset and link back to portfolio.
tests_needed: Trigger throw in dev, confirm branded fallback

history.notes: Addressed by app/error.tsx and global-error.tsx (same patch as f-2b8d3e6a).

findings.title: Engine view shows 'no routing config' when APIs fail
findings.description: On fetch failure, routing defaults to {} and UI shows 'no routing config found' even when config may exist. Indistinguishable from a real empty config.

proof_hooks.summary: catch swallows error; empty routes branch

findings.impact: Misleading diagnosis for operators debugging connectivity vs config.

suggested_fix.approach: Separate error state; show 'Could not load routing' with retry.
tests_needed: Mock failed routing fetch, assert error copy

history.notes: EngineView: routingError state; set when !routingRes.ok or catch; show 'Could not load routing' + Retry in routing section.

findings.title: Orchestration job dispatch errors are silent in the UI
findings.description: dispatchAction and enqueueWeekly throw on failure but callers do not catch; errors surface only in console. User sees loading ellipsis stop with no explanation.

proof_hooks.summary: throw new Error after !res.ok; onClick handlers have no try/catch UI

findings.impact: Operators cannot tell auth failure from network failure.

suggested_fix.approach: Catch errors; set toast or inline error state next to Run/enqueue buttons.
tests_needed: Mock 401 POST /api/orchestration/jobs, assert error message visible

history.notes: OrchestrationPanel: dispatchError state; catch in dispatchAction/enqueueWeekly; show inline error + dismiss.

findings.title: Inconsistent 'import' vs 'onboard' for the same flow
findings.description: Header button says 'import project'; modal title 'Onboard project'; CTA 'Onboard'; empty state says 'onboard project'. Same primary action uses three phrasings.

proof_hooks.summary: import project button
Onboard project / Onboard

findings.impact: Minor cognitive load; hurts polish and search/help consistency.

suggested_fix.approach: Pick one term (e.g. 'Onboard') and use across button, modal title, and empty state.

history.notes: Standardized on 'Onboard': page.tsx header and empty-state button now 'Onboard project'; ImportModal already used Onboard project/Onboard.

findings.title: Linear sync uses native alert for errors
findings.description: Push/pull failures call alert(), unlike other surfaces that use inline text. Jarring and inconsistent with app tone.

proof_hooks.summary: alert on push failure

findings.impact: Breaks flow on mobile/small windows; feels unpolished.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace with inline error banner matching ImportModal/FindingDetail patterns.

history.notes: LinearSync: syncError state; inline error banner (red) for push/pull failures; removed alert().

findings.title: Findings search field has no programmatic label
findings.description: Input uses placeholder 'search findings…' only. Labels should be associated via htmlFor/id or aria-label for screen readers.

proof_hooks.summary: Bare text input, no aria-label

findings.impact: Screen reader users may not understand the field purpose.

suggested_fix.approach: Add aria-label='Search findings' or visible label with htmlFor.
tests_needed: axe or manual SR check

history.notes: ProjectView: added aria-label='Search findings' to search input.

findings.title: Custom role=button cards handle Enter but not Space
findings.description: NextActionCard, FindingRow, ProjectCard use role=button and onKeyDown for Enter only. WCAG expects Space to activate actionable elements.

proof_hooks.summary: Enter-only key handler

findings.impact: Keyboard users who use Space may think control is broken.

suggested_fix.approach: Handle Space (prevent scroll) or use native <button> layout.
tests_needed: Keyboard: Tab to card, Space activates

history.notes: NextActionCard, FindingRow, ProjectCard: onKeyDown handles Space (preventDefault + activate) in addition to Enter.

findings.title: Product voice undefined: Title Case nav vs lowercase CTAs
findings.description: Sidebar uses 'Portfolio', 'Engine', 'Agents' in title case; primary actions use lowercase ('import project', 'sync now'). No documented voice guide. Recommend: one sentence-level rule (e.g. 'UI chrome: Title Case; actions: sentence case' or all lowercase for tool aesthetic).

proof_hooks.summary: Compare Shell nav labels vs page primary buttons

findings.impact: Inconsistent future copy without a single source of truth.

suggested_fix.approach: Add docs/VOICE.md or CONTRIBUTING UX section; align Shell + page.tsx buttons in one pass.

history.notes: Added docs/VOICE.md (nav/chrome Title Case; actions sentence case). Shell and page.tsx already aligned; README links to VOICE.md.

findings.title: logic agent JSON omitted run_metadata; proof_hooks non-schema
findings.description: logic-20260318-120000.json lacked run_metadata and used history_events / proof_hooks without hook_type. Findings merged after synthesizer normalization; re-export for audit trail.

proof_hooks.summary: Source file missing required run_metadata

findings.impact: Weaker provenance on logic audit run metadata.

suggested_fix.approach: Re-run runtime-bug-hunter emitting full v1.1.0 agent_output.

next_actions.action: Add RLS (or documented server-only DB roles) for lyra_projects, lyra_audit_jobs, lyra_audit_runs
next_actions.rationale: Largest blast radius if Supabase keys or PostgREST surface is misused.

next_actions.action: Add FK from job/run project_name to lyra_projects(name) after data cleanup
Extend LyraAuditRunRow + rowRun to include payload
next_actions.rationale: Quick win; aligns types with schema.

next_actions.action: Align job_type DB constraint with LyraJobType
next_actions.rationale: Defense in depth for orchestration pipeline.

next_actions.action: Add minimal GitHub Actions workflow: lint + tsc --noEmit + build for dashboard
next_actions.rationale: Highest leverage to catch breakage before deploy.

next_actions.action: Add app/error.tsx (and global-error if needed) for Next.js dashboard
next_actions.rationale: Major UX and recovery gap for any runtime error.

next_actions.action: Resolve Netlify vs Vercel canonical deploy and document
next_actions.rationale: Unblocks consistent CI/CD and secrets targeting.

top_fixes.why_now: P0 blocker — Dashboard APIs allow unauthenticated read/write of projects and findings
top_fixes.risk: Full confidentiality and integrity loss for audit data; possible destruction of audit history.

top_fixes.why_now: P0 major — Supabase tables lyra_* created with no RLS or policies
top_fixes.risk: Cross-tenant or public exposure if tables are ever queried with a role subject to RLS defaults, or if anon/service keys leak; no database-level isolation for multi-tenant scenarios.

top_fixes.why_now: P0 major — No GitHub Actions (or other) CI pipeline
top_fixes.risk: Broken builds or type errors can reach Netlify/production without review; regressions discovered only after deploy or manual runs.

top_fixes.why_now: P0 major — Next.js App Router has no route or global error boundary
top_fixes.risk: Users see generic error states; no consistent branded recovery or error reporting hook from the UI tree.

top_fixes.why_now: P1 major — No dedicated health check endpoint for load balancers or uptime
top_fixes.risk: Uptime monitors and deploy verification rely on full pages; DB/API failures may be harder to distinguish from static success.

top_fixes.why_now: P1 major — GET /api/orchestration/jobs exposes job and run history without authentication
top_fixes.risk: Operational metadata leak; aids targeted abuse.

top_fixes.why_now: P1 major — POST /api/sync/linear/push callable without auth; burns LINEAR_API_KEY
top_fixes.risk: Abuse of third-party API; unsolicited issues.

top_fixes.why_now: P1 major — Failed /api/projects shows empty portfolio with no error
top_fixes.risk: Users may believe data is lost or that onboarding is required; support burden and wrong actions.

top_fixes.why_now: P1 major — lyra_audit_jobs.project_name not FK-referenced to lyra_projects
top_fixes.risk: Orphan job references, confusing orchestration UI, and worker behavior when project missing.

top_fixes.why_now: P1 major — One OpenAI chat completion per app in a single audit job multiplies token cost
top_fixes.risk: Portfolio-wide or multi-app jobs can produce 3–10× API spend versus batched or single-pass strategies; also increases wall-clock time.

commit_plan.title: fix(audit): address P0 data, deploy, and worker reliability
steps: Add RLS migration or document server-only DB + restrict roles
Add GitHub Actions: lint, tsc --noEmit, build
Protect /api/* with shared secret or auth middleware
Wrap process-job post-claim path in try/finally to completeJob on failure
tests_or_checks: npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit in dashboard
Policy/integration test for RLS if enabled
Worker unit test: loadPrompts throws after claim

regression_checklist: Dashboard: npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build
Worker: npm run build && smoke processJob happy path
API: curl orchestration routes with/without secret returns expected 401/200
DB: if RLS added, verify anon cannot read lyra_*
UI: trigger intentional React error; branded error boundary appears

reaudit_plan.reason: Did not exhaust every API route or static asset; focused on hot paths (projects API, worker job loop, LLM calls, orchestration fetches).
Worker process, hosting-specific hardening, and all client components for secret handling not exhaustively reviewed; CORS assumed Next defaults.
